item.title: OpenAI projects $2.5 billion in ad revenue this year, $100 billion by 2030, Axios reports
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI expects to generate $2.5 billion in advertising revenue this year, with projections to reach $100 billion by ​2030, Axios reported on Thursday, citing a source ‌familiar with recent presentations to investors.

item.title: Drone maker AEVEX eyes $2.35 billion valuation in US IPO
item.description: Private equity-backed ​drone maker AEVEX said on ‌Thursday it was targeting a valuation of up to $2.35 billion in its U.S ​initial public offering, the latest ​defense firm looking to capitalize ⁠on investor appetite.

item.title: Amazon to stock Lilly's new weight-loss pill at US kiosks, offer same-day delivery
item.description: Amazon.com said on Thursday its pharmacy unit will stock Eli Lilly's new weight-loss pill at kiosks located at some of the company's primary care ​clinics and offer same-day delivery of the drug.

item.title: Swiss-based Terra Quantum plans to list on Nasdaq via SPAC at $3.25 billion valuation
item.description: Terra Quantum said on Thursday it plans to list on ​Nasdaq this year through a merger with a U.S. special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) ‌in a deal that would value the German-Swiss quantum technology company at $3.25 billion.

item.title: Chevron forecasts up to $2.2 billion boost to upstream earnings from higher Q1 prices
item.description: Chevron said on Thursday its upstream earnings were expected ​to rise by $1.6 billion to $2.2 billion ‌in the first quarter versus the fourth quarter of 2025, driven by surging oil and gas ​prices from volatility linked to the Iran war.

item.title: CoreWeave signs $21 billion AI cloud deal with Meta
item.description: CoreWeave said on ​Thursday it has ‌entered into an expanded agreement to ​provide Meta ​Platforms with $21 billion in ⁠cloud capacity, as ​the social media ​giant scales its infrastructure to support increasingly ​complex AI workloads.

item.title: BlackBerry forecasts upbeat quarterly revenue, says turnaround complete
item.description: BlackBerry on Thursday forecast first-quarter revenue above estimates, as the Canadian software company's turnaround gains traction on the back of ​strong demand across its cybersecurity and embedded software divisions.

item.title: Exclusive: North American pension funds stick with private credit bets
item.description: Several big North American pension systems are standing by their private credit holdings even as the sector ​faces choppy waters, a sign of both the industry's potential resilience and its long reach into workers' retirement plans.

item.title: EV maker Polestar's quarterly sales rise as Europe momentum continues
item.description: Sweden's Polestar reported a 7% rise in first-quarter sales volume on Thursday, as the EV maker benefitted from ​its Europe-focused strategy to drive demand amid tariff-driven uncertainty ‌and high costs.

item.title: Dubai’s DAE and Blackstone launch $1.6 billion annual aircraft leasing programme
item.description: Dubai Aerospace Enterprise and Blackstone Credit and Insurance have agreed ​to launch a joint aircraft leasing ‌investment programme targeting annual deployment of approximately $1.6 billion, the companies said on Thursday.

item.title: Morning Bid: Relief rally hits pause
item.description: A look at the day ahead in U.S. and global markets by Amanda Cooper

item.title: India's TCS beats fourth quarter view as North America holds up
item.description: India's top software services exporter Tata Consultancy Services beat profit and revenue estimates for the fourth ​quarter on Thursday, helped by strength in its North American ‌market and a falling domestic currency.

item.title: Amazon cloud unit's AI revenue run rate exceeds $15 billion in first quarter, CEO says
item.description: Amazon.com said on Thursday its cloud ​business's AI revenue run rate was ‌more than $15 billion in the first quarter of 2026, the company's first ​disclosure of direct financial returns ​from its artificial intelligence efforts.

item.title: Pentagon's ouster of Anthropic opens doors for small AI rivals
item.description: Small defense industry artificial intelligence startups are suddenly fielding calls from generals, combatant commanders and deep-pocketed investors, after the souring relationship between the Pentagon and its ​once-favored AI vendor, Anthropic, reinforced the need to diversify and increase the number of AI providers for the military.

item.title: Exclusive: Chinese firm hired lobbyists with ties to Don Jr, then scored a win in Washington
item.description: A lobbying firm led by one of Donald Trump Jr.'s hunting buddies helped a Chinese company make its case to a U.S. national security watchdog against a U.S. startup that was seeking to have the Chinese firm removed as one of its investors, public filings show.

item.title: Exclusive: Tesla is developing a new smaller, cheaper EV
item.description: Tesla is developing an all-new smaller, cheaper electric SUV, four people familiar with the matter told Reuters.

item.title: Exclusive: Iran war doubles Russia's main oil revenue to $9 billion in April
item.description: The Reuters calculation is some of the first concrete evidence of a windfall ​for Russia, the world's second-largest oil exporter, from the Iran war, which oil traders say has ​triggered the most serious energy crisis in recent history.

item.title: US stock futures dip over shaky Mideast truce; inflation in focus
item.description: U.S. stock futures inched lower on Thursday after ​the indexes rallied in the previous session, as cracks emerged in the fragile Middle East ceasefire, ‌while investors turned their focus to a domestic inflation reading later in the day.

item.title: US container imports down 1% in March, Descartes says
item.description: U.S. container import volumes fell ​1.1% in March from last ‌year, supply chain technology provider Descartes Systems Group said on Thursday.

item.title: Mercedes Q1 sales down in tough 'transition year' for China business
item.description: Mercedes-Benz reported a sales slump at the start of 2026 as the ​tough Chinese market continued to pressure the German ‌premium carmaker during an overhaul of its model lineup to regain lost ground.

item.title: China's car sales extend declines in March
item.description: China's car sales fell for a sixth ​straight month in March, ‌as rising fuel prices hit demand for gasoline models while ​electric vehicle sales ​still reeled from reduced incentives ⁠as the economic recovery ​is sputtering.

item.title: Seven & i delays listing of North America business
item.description: Seven & i Holdings said on Thursday it has pushed back the timing ​of the listing of its North ‌American operations to the financial year starting April 2027 or later, rather than in the second ​half of 2026.

item.title: UniCredit flags risk of losing key staff, clients in a Commerzbank tie-up
item.description: Italian bank UniCredit said the uncertainty arising from an integration with Commerzbank could cause employees "with fundamental institutional knowledge" ​to leave, potentially leading to client loss for ‌both lenders.

item.title: Uniqlo owner Fast Retailing books 29.4% rise in Q2 profit, raises forecast
item.description: The Japanese owner of clothing brand Uniqlo said on Thursday that quarterly profit rose 29.4% just before the ​Middle East crisis roiled global markets and supply chains, and raised its full-year forecast ‌on strong international growth.

item.title: British American Tobacco brings back former executive Constantinescu as CFO
item.description: British American Tobacco on Thursday appointed Dragos Constantinescu ​as its chief financial officer, bringing ‌back a former executive who had spent 16 years at the tobacco giant before moving ​to Asahi Breweries.

item.title: China's March bank lending seen surging on post-holiday rebound
item.description: Chinese banks likely extended significantly more new loans in March than in February, according to a Reuters poll on Thursday, driven by improved credit demand ​and a seasonal rebound.

item.title: Morning Bid: There's a Gulf between market hopes and reality
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Wayne Cole.

item.title: South Korea's Kia cuts 2030 EV target over 20%, plans humanoid robots at U.S. factory
item.description: South Korea's Kia Corp said on Thursday that ​it aims to sell 1 ‌million electric vehicles (EVs) by 2030, lowering its earlier ​target by about 20% ​from 1.26 million announced last ⁠year, reflecting weaker ​EV demand and the impact ​of the U.S. scrapping EV subsidies last year.

item.title: OVHcloud launches new unit to meet demand from European militaries
item.description: France's OVHcloud is creating a ‌dedicated defence vertical after several European defence ministries approached it to support their military ​digital transformation, the datacentre operator said ​on Thursday.

item.title: Arm CEO Haas in line to lead much of parent SoftBank's international business, FT reports
item.description: Chip designer Arm's CEO Rene Haas is in line to lead ​much of parent SoftBank Group's international business ‌while keeping his current role at Arm, the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing multiple people familiar with the ​matter.
